why.title: E-invoicing is becoming <hl>mandatory</hl> in the UAE
why.subtitle: The UAE is rolling out mandatory e-invoicing under the Ministry of Finance's Electronic Invoicing System. Businesses must exchange invoices through an Accredited Service Provider (ASP) using the Peppol PINT AE standard.
why.point1Body: Switching ASPs mid-implementation means re-integrating your ERP, retraining your team and re-testing compliance. Getting it right the first time matters.
why.point2Body: Provider pricing ranges from affordable SME bundles to enterprise contracts. The right choice depends on your invoice volume, systems and budget.
why.point3Title: We do the matching for you
why.point3Body: Tell us your requirements and budget. Our team compares the pre-approved providers and connects you with the one that fits — at no cost to you.

directoryPreview.title: The complete pre-approved provider list
directoryPreview.subtitle: Every provider recognised by the UAE Ministry of Finance, kept current.

how.title: How it works
how.step1Body: Share your company size, invoice volume, current software and budget — takes 2 minutes.
how.step2Body: Our team evaluates the pre-approved ASPs against your requirements and price point.
how.step3Body: We introduce you to the best-fit provider. No obligation, no fees for your business.

quiz.metaTitle: UAE E-Invoicing Readiness Check — Free 2-Minute Assessment
quiz.metaDescription: Is your business ready for mandatory UAE e-invoicing? Take the free 2-minute readiness assessment and get a personalised action plan plus a free provider match.
quiz.title: E-Invoicing Readiness Check
quiz.subtitle: Answer {total} quick questions to see how ready your business is for the UAE e-invoicing mandate — and what to do next.

q1.text: Does your business currently issue invoices electronically (not PDF-by-email)?

q2.text: What accounting or ERP system do you use?
q2.a1: A major system (SAP, Oracle, Dynamics, Zoho, Tally…)

q3.text: Are you registered for VAT / Corporate Tax in the UAE?

q4.text: Have you heard of the Peppol PINT AE invoice format?
q4.a1: Yes, and we know what it requires
q4.a2: Heard of it, but unclear on details

q5.text: Do you have a budget allocated for e-invoicing compliance?

results.title: Your readiness score

high.label: Well prepared
high.body: Your business has strong foundations. Your main task now is choosing the right Accredited Service Provider and completing integration and testing before your go-live date.

medium.label: Getting there
medium.body: You have some building blocks in place, but there are gaps to close — most businesses at this stage benefit from an ASP that offers guided onboarding and ERP integration support.

low.label: Early days
low.body: You're at the start of the journey — and that's fine, but the clock is ticking. The right ASP can handle most of the complexity for you. Start the conversation early.

results.cta: Now tell us your requirements below and we'll match you with the provider that fits your situation and budget — free.
results.trackBody: We saved your details — a specialist will follow up within one business day. Follow the status anytime:
results.moreDetail: Want a sharper match? Add budget & system details

gate.title: Your results are ready

about.metaTitle: About Us — UAE E-Invoicing Providers Directory
about.metaDescription: Who is behind the UAE e-invoicing provider directory, what we do, and how our free provider matching works.
about.title: About this directory
about.body1: We maintain the most complete and current independent directory of e-invoicing service providers pre-approved under the UAE Ministry of Finance's Electronic Invoicing System accreditation framework.
about.body2: Choosing an Accredited Service Provider is one of the most consequential compliance decisions a UAE business will make in the coming years. Pricing models, ERP integrations, onboarding quality and support vary enormously between providers — and comparing them one by one takes weeks.
about.body3: That's why we offer free provider matching: tell us your invoice volume, systems and budget, and our team shortlists the providers that genuinely fit. We're independent, we're not owned by any provider, and using our matching service is free for businesses.
about.howWeKeepCurrent: How we keep the directory current
about.howWeKeepCurrentBody: The directory is reviewed and refreshed continuously against official and public sources, and every entry shows when the directory was last updated. If you spot an inaccuracy, tell us and we'll fix it promptly.

registry.metaTitle: UAE E-Invoicing ASP Registry: {count} MoF Pre-Approved Providers & Contacts ({year})
registry.metaDescription: The full registry of all {count} UAE Ministry of Finance pre-approved e-invoicing service providers with official contact persons, emails and phone numbers. Updated {date}.
registry.title: UAE E-Invoicing Service Provider Registry
registry.subtitle: All {count} pre-approved eInvoicing Service Providers with their official contact details, in alphabetical order. Updated {date}.
registry.intro1: Under Article 15 of Ministerial Decision No. 64 of 2025 on the eligibility criteria and accreditation procedure for Service Providers under the Electronic Invoicing System, the UAE Ministry of Finance maintains a list of pre-approved eInvoicing Service Providers. Final accreditation is granted in accordance with Article 16 of the same Decision.
registry.intro2: The registry below mirrors that official list, including each provider's designated contact person(s), email and phone number, and is refreshed regularly — the current version is shown as of the date above. This is an independent directory; always verify a provider's status with the Ministry of Finance before contracting.

matchCta.title: Don't want to call {count} providers one by one?
matchCta.body: Tell us your requirements and budget once — our team shortlists the right providers for you and makes the introduction. Free for businesses.

calculator.title: Penalty calculator
calculator.body: Model your AED exposure under Cabinet Decision No. 106 of 2025 — appointment delays, missed go-live, invoices issued outside the system.

planner.title: Readiness planner
planner.body: A dated milestone plan for your phase, worked backwards from your go-live date and adjusted to your systems.

checklist.title: Compliance checklist
checklist.body: Every step from scoping to go-live in 23 checkable items. Progress saves in your browser and prints cleanly.

assessment.title: Readiness assessment
assessment.body: Five questions, an instant readiness score, and personalised next steps for your business.

pint.title: PINT AE field reference
pint.body: A searchable reference of all 88 invoice fields, UAE document type codes and transaction flags.

hub.ctaTitle: Prefer a human to do the work?
hub.ctaBody: Tell us your systems, volume and budget — we compare every pre-approved provider and introduce you to the right fit, free.

calculator.metaDescription: Calculate your UAE e-invoicing penalty exposure: AED 5,000/month for missing the ASP deadline or go-live, AED 100 per invoice outside the system, AED 1,000/day for unreported changes.
calculator.title: E-invoicing penalty calculator
calculator.subtitle: Estimate what non-compliance would cost your business under Cabinet Decision No. 106 of 2025. Adjust the scenario — the totals update instantly.
calculator.phaseDates: Appoint an ASP by {appoint} · go live by {goLive}
calculator.monthsNotLive: Months past go-live without the system
calculator.invoicesOutside: Invoices issued outside the system (per month)
calculator.daysUnreported: Days of unreported changes to registered data
calculator.totalNote: Cumulative administrative penalties for this scenario

rules.outside: Invoices outside the system (AED 100 each, capped)

calculator.capNote: The per-invoice penalty is capped at AED 5,000 per calendar month — your volume hits the cap.
calculator.ctaTitle: The fix costs less than one month of penalties
calculator.ctaBody: Appointing an accredited provider stops the clock. We shortlist the right one for your systems and budget, free.
calculator.disclaimer: Estimates reflect Cabinet Decision No. 106 of 2025 and implementation decisions as amended at the time of writing. Penalties apply from your phase's mandatory dates; nothing applies during voluntary adoption. This tool is general information, not legal or tax advice — verify current amounts with official MoF/FTA publications.

planner.metaTitle: UAE E-Invoicing Readiness Planner: Your Dated Milestone Plan
planner.metaDescription: Generate a dated e-invoicing implementation plan for your business: ASP shortlisting, appointment, integration, testing and go-live milestones worked backwards from your phase deadline.
planner.title: E-invoicing readiness planner
planner.subtitle: Answer three questions and get a dated milestone plan, worked backwards from your phase's go-live date.

shortlist.title: Shortlist providers
shortlist.body: Compare pre-approved ASPs on integration fit, pricing and onboarding capacity. Two to four weeks is enough with a focused shortlist.

appoint.title: Appoint your ASP (target)
appoint.body: Sign with your chosen provider — this stops the penalty clock and books your onboarding slot before the pre-deadline rush.

appointDeadline.title: Official appointment deadline
appointDeadline.body: The legal deadline to have an accredited provider appointed. Missing it costs AED 5,000 per month.

integrate.title: Integrate & clean data
integrate.body: Connect your systems to the ASP and clean master data in parallel: customer TRNs, VAT codes, addresses, rounding rules.

test.title: Test end-to-end
test.body: Validate real invoice samples across your actual scenarios — credit notes, discounts, multi-currency — and train the finance team.

goLive.title: Mandatory go-live
goLive.body: From this date, in-scope invoices must be issued and exchanged through the Electronic Invoicing System.

planner.highVolumeNote: At your volume, ask shortlisted providers about throughput guarantees, batch APIs and peak-period handling — and include a realistic volume test in UAT.
planner.ctaTitle: Want this plan executed for you?
planner.ctaBody: We track every pre-approved provider's integration coverage and onboarding capacity. Tell us your setup and we'll shortlist the best fit, free.

checklist.metaTitle: UAE E-Invoicing Compliance Checklist: 23 Steps to Go-Live
checklist.metaDescription: The complete UAE e-invoicing compliance checklist: scoping, ASP selection, data cleanup, integration, testing and go-live in 23 checkable steps that save in your browser.
checklist.title: E-invoicing compliance checklist
checklist.subtitle: Every step from scoping to go-live. Your progress saves automatically in this browser, and the list prints cleanly for project meetings.

scope.title: Scope & phase
scope.subtitle: Know exactly which rules and dates apply to you.
scope.i1: Confirm your annual revenue against the AED 50 million threshold to identify your phase
scope.i2: Diarise both of your dates: the ASP appointment deadline and the go-live date
scope.i3: Map which of your transactions are in scope (B2B and B2G; note exclusions with your tax adviser)
scope.i4: Brief leadership on the mandate, penalties and the project budget

select.title: Select your ASP
select.subtitle: The decision that shapes everything downstream.
select.i1: List your invoicing systems, versions and monthly invoice volumes (issued and received)
select.i2: Shortlist pre-approved providers with proven integrations for your systems
select.i3: Request demos and written onboarding start dates from your shortlist
select.i4: Compare pricing on your real volume, including onboarding and support tiers
select.i5: Sign with your chosen provider before the appointment deadline

data.title: Prepare your data
data.subtitle: Master data quality decides how smooth go-live is.
data.i1: Collect and verify TRNs for every active B2B customer
data.i2: Map every product and service line to its correct VAT treatment
data.i3: Standardise customer names and addresses against registration records
data.i4: Archive dormant customers and duplicate records
data.i5: Agree rounding and totals rules between your system and the ASP

integrate.title: Integrate & test
integrate.subtitle: Connect, validate, and rehearse before the switch.
integrate.i1: Connect your systems to the ASP (connector, API or portal)
integrate.i2: Run validation on real invoice samples and fix every rejection cause
integrate.i3: Test edge cases: credit notes, discounts, multi-currency, summary invoices
integrate.i4: Test received (inbound) invoices, not just issued ones
integrate.i5: Train the finance team on the new flow and failure handling

live.title: Go live & operate
live.subtitle: Switch on, monitor, and stay compliant.
live.i1: Go live on or before your mandatory date
live.i2: Monitor delivery and FTA reporting status daily in the first weeks
live.i3: Set up a process to notify your ASP of changes to registered data (AED 1,000/day if unreported)
live.i4: Review invoice rejection rates monthly and fix root causes

checklist.ctaBody: It's the hardest checkbox on the list. We compare all pre-approved providers against your systems and budget, free.

pint.metaTitle: PINT AE Field Reference: All 88 UAE E-Invoice Fields
pint.metaDescription: Searchable PINT AE reference for the UAE e-invoice format: all 88 fields with UBL paths and obligations, document type codes (380, 381, 480, 81) and the 8-digit transaction flag string.
pint.title: PINT AE field reference
pint.subtitle: The UAE e-invoice format, searchable: document type codes, the BTAE-02 transaction flag string, and all 88 fields with their UBL paths and obligations.
pint.docTypesSubtitle: The invoice type code that identifies a UAE document.
pint.flagsSubtitle: UAE invoices carry an 8-digit flag string in ProfileExecutionID. Each position is 1 if it applies, else 0 — e.g. 10000000 for a Free Trade Zone supply, 00000000 for none.
pint.fieldsSubtitle: International terms use EN 16931 / PINT identifiers (IBT-xxx); UAE-specific terms use BTAE-xx. Search by code, name, UBL path or description.
pint.searchPlaceholder: Search fields, BT codes, UBL paths…
pint.sourceNote: Source: Peppol International (PINT) model for Billing, UAE specialisation — {version}. Obligations and conditions follow the model and UAE business rules; always validate against the current official MoF / Peppol specification before relying on any field.
